Remediation plan for finding sg-finding-git-001
  Secret kind: GitHub Token
  Severity:    high
  Preview:     ghp_...7890
  Fingerprint: sha256:aabbccddee11

Steps:

  1. Rotate or revoke the credential immediately
     Before rewriting history, ensure the compromised credential is rotated or revoked at the provider. This limits the window of exposure even if the history rewrite is delayed.

  2. Create a fresh mirror clone
     Clone a bare mirror of the repository to work on. This preserves all refs and avoids affecting the working directory.
     $ git clone --mirror <repository-url> repo-mirror

  3. Prepare a replacement text file
     Create a replacement text file for git filter-repo. List the secret pattern without including the raw secret in the output.
     $ echo 'ghp_* => [REVOKED]' > /tmp/replacements.txt

  4. Run git filter-repo manually
     Execute git filter-repo in the mirror clone directory. The --replace-text option will replace all occurrences of the replacement patterns across the entire history.
     $ cd repo-mirror && git filter-repo --force --replace-text /tmp/replacements.txt

  5. Re-scan the rewritten history
     After the rewrite, run SecretGuard on the mirror clone to verify no secrets remain in the history.
     $ secretguard scan repo-mirror --git-history --format json

  6. Force-push only after team coordination
     Rewriting history requires everyone to rebase onto the new history. Coordinate with the team before force-pushing. All local clones will need to be re-cloned or rebased.
     $ cd repo-mirror && git push --force --mirror origin

  ⚠ Do not execute git filter-repo on the original clone — work on the mirror only.

  ⚠ Force-pushing a rewritten branch will break all open pull requests and local clones.

  ⚠ Ensure all team members have rebased before force-pushing.
